description_long: This is a weathered stone obelisk, partially broken and missing its upper section, standing atop a multi-tiered rectangular base. The obelisk is composed of rough, light-colored stone with visible cracks and fractures, suggesting significant age and exposure to the elements. The base features a combination of smooth, carved stone layers and rougher, more irregular stone blocks, with some sections appearing restored or reconstructed. One face of the obelisk displays faint, inscribed text, likely of historical or commemorative significance, though much of it is eroded and difficult to read. The overall appearance is monumental and solemn, evoking the style of ancient or classical memorials often found in urban parks or plazas. There are no visible decorative motifs beyond the inscriptions and the stepped design of the base.
description: A broken, weathered stone obelisk with faint inscriptions, mounted on a multi-tiered rectangular base of both smooth and rough stone.
description_view_0: Front view shows the fractured obelisk with a prominent, partially restored base and the main inscribed face.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the deep cracks and irregular stonework, with less visible inscription and more exposed rough stone.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the plain, fractured surface of the obelisk and the layered structure of the base.
description_view_3: Back view displays the most extensive area of inscription, with the stepped base and visible restoration work.
attribute_inscription: Faint, weathered inscriptions are visible on the front and back faces of the obelisk, carved in a blocky, ancient script. The text is eroded and only partially legible.
attribute_base_design: The base is multi-tiered, rectangular, and constructed from both smooth, carved stone and rough, irregular stone blocks. Some sections appear restored, with clean lines and sharp edges contrasting with the weathered stone.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is broken and irregular, lacking any decorative features or finishing elements.
